channel.title: The Weekly Challenge - RSS Feed
channel.description: Get regular The Weekly Challenge RSS Feed.

item.title: RECAP - The Weekly Challenge - 360
item.description: Thank you Team PWC for your continuous support and encouragement.

item.title: The Weekly Challenge - 362
item.description: Welcome to the Week #362 of The Weekly Challenge.

item.title: RECAP - The Weekly Challenge - 361
item.description: Thank you Team PWC for your continuous support and encouragement.

item.title: The Weekly Challenge - Guest Contributions
item.description: As you know, The Weekly Challenge, primarily focus on Perl and Raku. During the Week #018, we received solutions to The Weekly Challenge - 018 by Orestis Zekai in Python. It was pleasant surprise to receive solutions in something other than Perl and Raku. Ever since regular team members also started contributing in other languages like Ada, APL, Awk, BASIC, Bash, Bc, Befunge-93, Bourne Shell, BQN, Brainfuck, C3, C, CESIL, Chef, COBOL, Coconut, C Shell, C++, Clojure, Crystal, CUDA, D, Dart, Dc, Elixir, Elm, Emacs Lisp, Erlang, Excel VBA, F#, Factor, Fennel, Fish, Forth, Fortran, Gembase, Gleam, GNAT, Go, GP, Groovy, Haskell, Haxe, HTML, Hy, Idris, IO, J, Janet, Java, JavaScript, Julia, K, Kap, Korn Shell, Kotlin, Lisp, Logo, Lua, M4, Maxima, Miranda, Modula 3, MMIX, Mumps, Myrddin, Nelua, Nim, Nix, Node.js, Nuweb, Oberon, Octave, OCaml, Odin, Ook, Pascal, PHP, PicoLisp, Python, PostgreSQL, Postscript, PowerShell, Prolog, R, Racket, Rexx, Ring, Roc, Ruby, Rust, Scala, Scheme, Sed, Smalltalk, SQL, Standard ML, SVG, Swift, Tcl, TypeScript, Typst, Uiua, V, Visual BASIC, WebAssembly, Wolfram, XSLT, YaBasic and Zig.

item.title: The Weekly Challenge - 360

item.title: Mojolicious with WebSocket
item.description: As a longtime Dancer2 fan, Iâ€™ve always admired its elegance for building web apps. But recently, I decided to step out of my comfort zone and try something new, Mojolicious. Known for its realâ€‘time web capabilities, Mojo seemed like the perfect framework to experiment with WebSockets. And what better way to test it than building a live chat room?

item.title: RECAP - The Weekly Challenge - 358
item.description: Thank you Team PWC for your continuous support and encouragement.

item.title: Dancer2 + Dancer2::Plugin::DBIC::Async + HTMX
item.description: As you all know, I released a significant update (v0.50) to DBIx::Class::Async. The latest version included some breaking changes, which means I needed to revisit its plugin, Dancer2::Plugin::DBIC::Async. I’ve applied the necessary patches to ensure compatibility with the latest DBIx::Class::Async release. The updated plugin is now available as version v0.05.

item.title: RECAP - The Weekly Challenge - 357
item.description: Thank you Team PWC for your continuous support and encouragement.

item.title: Evolution of DBIx::Class::Async
item.description: How do you make the world’s best Perl ORM non-blocking without rewriting your entire schema? Weâ€™ve solved the “DBI Ceiling” by isolating DBIx::Class in a worker pool. Keep your existing Result classes and use $schema->await() to run queries without stopping your heartbeats.

item.title: DBIx::Class::Async - UPDATE
item.description: Few days ago, I shared a blog post about Introduction to DBIx::Class::Async. It was well received and got many encouraging comments about DBIx::Class::Async.

item.title: DBIx::Class::Async
item.description: This blog post marks the beginning of 2026 and also DBIx::Class::Async is the first distribution uploaded to CPAN in 2026.
